_comment: React 19 rules. Loaded when any frontend framework is detected (React is universal).

rules.message: React 19 passes ref as a prop — forwardRef is no longer needed. Use function MyComponent({ ref }) instead.
PropTypes removed in React 19. Use TypeScript for type checking.
defaultProps removed in React 19. Use ES6 default parameters: function Comp({ text = 'default' }).
String refs removed in React 19. Use ref callbacks or useRef.
Legacy Context API removed in React 19. Use React.createContext() + useContext().
Math.random() causes hydration mismatch — server and client generate different values. Use a seeded random, pass as prop from server, or move to useEffect/useState initializer.
Date.now() causes hydration mismatch — server and client timestamps differ. Use useEffect for client-only timestamps, or wrap in Suspense boundary.
crypto.randomUUID() causes hydration mismatch. Use React.useId() for stable IDs, or generate on server and pass as prop.

suppressHydrationWarning is an escape hatch, not a fix. It only works one level deep and hides real bugs. Fix the mismatch instead: use useEffect for client-only values, useId() for IDs, or pass dynamic values as props from the server.
